Super Bear Adventure Mod APK on Android 14 & 15 — Full Compatibility Guide

Install Super Bear Adventure Mod APK on Android 14 and Android 15. How to enable unknown sources, bypass Restricted Settings, and fix install errors on the latest Android versions.

June 28, 2026 Updated Jun 28, 2026 8 min read
Share:

Android 14 and Android 15 introduced additional security measures for sideloaded apps. This guide covers everything you need to know to install Super Bear Adventure Mod APK on the latest Android versions without errors.

Android 14 and 15 Compatibility

Super Bear Adventure Mod APK v12.1.5 is fully compatible with Android 14 and Android 15. The game’s Android target SDK and minimum SDK requirements are both met on these versions:

  • Minimum required: Android 5.1 (API level 22)
  • Tested on: Android 14 (API 34), Android 15 (API 35)
  • No compatibility patches required

The primary difference from older Android versions is the install process, not the game itself.

What Changed in Android 14 for APK Installs

Android 13 introduced Restricted Settings and Android 14 tightened the unknown sources flow. Here is what actually changed:

Unknown Sources Is Now Per-App

In Android 9 and earlier, “Unknown Sources” was a single toggle in Security settings. Since Android 10, it is per-app — each app that downloads APKs needs its own permission. On Android 14, this setting is at:

Settings → Apps → Special App Access → Install Unknown Apps

You need to find the specific app you used to download (usually Chrome, Firefox, or a file manager) and enable it there.

Restricted Settings

Android 13 introduced Restricted Settings, which prevents apps installed from unknown sources from accessing accessibility services or notification listener permissions. This is relevant for certain mod menus that use accessibility shortcuts.

For Super Bear Adventure Mod APK specifically, Restricted Settings does not block the install or gameplay — the game does not require accessibility permissions.

Android 15 Play Protect Prompt

Android 15 may show an additional Play Protect warning for APKs from unknown sources. This is a standard warning — tap “Install Anyway” to proceed. The mod APK is clean and the warning is not indicative of any actual threat.

Step-by-Step Install Guide for Android 14

Step 1: Download the APK

Download the APK from superbearadventuremod.io/download/ using Chrome or your mobile browser. The file is 221 MB — download on Wi-Fi for fastest transfer.

Step 2: Enable Unknown Sources for Your Browser

  1. Go to Settings
  2. Tap Apps (or Application Manager)
  3. Tap the three dots (⋮) → Special App Access
  4. Tap Install Unknown Apps
  5. Find Chrome (or the browser you used to download)
  6. Tap it → toggle Allow from this source ON

Step 3: Open the APK

  1. Pull down the notification bar
  2. Tap the completed download notification
  3. OR open Files app → Downloads → tap super-bear-adventure-mod-apk.apk

Step 4: Handle Security Prompts

If you see “Install blocked for security”:

  • Tap the notification → it redirects you to the Unknown Apps settings
  • Enable the permission for your browser → go back and retry

If you see “Restricted Settings” warning:

  • Tap Learn more → Settings
  • Find the APK you just tried to install in Apps list
  • Tap the three-dot menu → Allow Restricted Settings
  • This only applies if the mod menu requires accessibility access

Step 5: Install

  1. Tap Install on the install prompt
  2. Wait for the installation to complete (10–30 seconds)
  3. Tap Done or Open
  4. Game launches with all mod features active

Android 14-Specific Device Issues

Pixel 8 / Pixel 9

Google Pixel devices on Android 14+ enforce Play Protect most strictly. If Play Protect blocks the install:

  1. Open Google Play Store
  2. Tap your profile icon → Play Protect
  3. Tap the gear icon → disable Scan apps with Play Protect (temporarily)
  4. Install the APK
  5. Re-enable Play Protect after install

The game will run correctly with Play Protect re-enabled — the runtime scan after install will pass.

OnePlus / OxygenOS 14

OxygenOS 14 combines Restricted Settings with additional OEM security. Go to:

Settings → Security → More Security Settings → Install Unknown Apps

The path differs slightly from stock Android. Find your browser in this menu.

Motorola / Android 14

Motorola devices running Android 14 have a streamlined unknown sources flow. When you tap the APK, the system automatically asks if you want to enable the permission for that source — tap Allow and installation proceeds immediately.

Android 15 Differences

Android 15 introduced Photo and Video permission changes but these do not affect game installs. The APK install flow is nearly identical to Android 14:

  1. Enable unknown sources per-app (same location as Android 14)
  2. Download and tap the APK
  3. Accept the enhanced security warning (“Install Anyway”)
  4. Install completes

Android 15 is still rolling out at time of writing. If you encounter an Android 15-specific issue not covered here, check our troubleshooting guide.

Troubleshooting Android 14 Install Errors

Error MessageSolution
”Install blocked for security”Enable unknown sources for your browser in Settings → Apps → Special App Access
”App not installed”Insufficient storage space — free up 500 MB minimum
”There is a problem parsing the package”Corrupted download — re-download the APK from /download/
“App not installed as package appears to be invalid”Old version conflict — uninstall existing version first if major version change
Play Protect blocking installTemporarily disable Play Protect (see Pixel section above)

Download and Install Now

Download the verified mod APK from superbearadventuremod.io/download/. The file is safe (0/72 VirusTotal detections) and fully compatible with Android 14 and 15.

For Samsung-specific install guidance, see the Samsung install guide. For Xiaomi/MIUI devices, see the Xiaomi guide.
